title: Face Recognition: Identifying Who It Is
concept: Face recognition compares facial features to a database to identify a specific person, either for verification (1:1) or identification (1:N).

exercises.prompt: Recap: What does a face detection model do?

options.text: It finds the presence and location of faces in an image.
It matches faces to names in a database.
It predicts the age of the person.

exercises.explain: Correct! Face detection is only responsible for finding where faces are in an image.

cards.title: Unlocking your phone
cards.body: When you look at your phone, it doesn't just recognize that a human is looking at the screen. It determines if **you** — the registered owner — are the one looking. This goes beyond simple detection into the realm of recognition.

cards.title: What is Face Recognition?
cards.body: Face Recognition is the task of matching a detected face against a database of known faces to determine identity. It operates in two main modes: Verification (1:1 match: is this person who they claim to be?) and Identification (1:N match: who is this person?).

cards.title: ID check vs. Roll call
cards.body: Verification (1:1) is like showing your passport to a border guard: they compare your face directly to the passport photo. Identification (1:N) is like a police officer searching a stack of mugshots to see if a suspect matches anyone in the records.

cards.title: Airport security gates
cards.body: Airports use 1:1 face verification to speed up check-in. The system captures your face at the gate and matches it against the digital photo stored in your passport chip to verify your identity.

cards.title: They are not the same pipeline
cards.body: People often use 'face detection' and 'face recognition' interchangeably. In practice, they are separate models in a serial pipeline: the detector first crops the face, and the recognition model then analyzes it.

cards.title: Verification vs. Identification
cards.body: Comparing the two core recognition tasks:

left.text:
Is this Alice?
Compares 1 face to 1 record
Fast, highly secure

right.text:
Who is this person?
Searches face against N records
Slower, scales with database size

exercises.prompt: Which task represents a 1:1 facial verification process?

options.text: Unlocking a smartphone using your face
Tagging ten friends in a group photo
Finding all faces in a video stream
Counting people at a stadium entrance

exercises.explain: Smartphone face unlock compares the user's face to the owner's stored face template (1:1 comparison).

exercises.prompt: True or False: Facial identification searches a captured face against a database of many known faces.
exercises.explain: True. Facial identification (1:N) matches a face against a database of N entries to identify the person.

exercises.sentence: Comparing a face to a single passport photo is an example of facial ___.
exercises.explain: Facial verification performs a 1:1 match to confirm a claimed identity.

options.text: An office lobby camera checking a visitor's face against all employee profiles to log them in
A camera autofocusing on a baby's face
A face-swap filter applying makeup on a selfie

exercises.explain: Matching a visitor against all employee profiles (one-to-many) is face identification.

exercises.prompt: You are designing an entry system for a gym. Members scan their barcode, and the camera verifies that the person entering matches the barcode owner's file photo. How should you structure this system?

options.text: Use the barcode to look up the stored photo, then run a 1:1 face verification model comparing the live face to the stored photo.
Search the live face against the gym's entire member database of 10,000 photos every time.
Only use face detection to count if a person is standing there.
Use an image classifier to detect if the gym lobby is empty.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Using the barcode provides a claimed identity, allowing you to run a fast, accurate 1:1 verification instead of searching the entire database. Fantastic! Next: a direct comparison of detection vs. recognition.
